data_in.description: Access to conda package manager and Bioconda channel

data_out.description: Installation verification report documenting successful conda installation and module import test results

domain_knowledge: Pyteomics requires Python 3 and all dependencies are optional, allowing selective installation of only required packages.
The Bioconda channel is a conda distribution channel specializing in bioinformatics packages and provides an alternative installation method to pip.
Core Pyteomics modules (pyteomics.mass, pyteomics.pepxml, pyteomics.mzid) provide essential proteomics data analysis functionality and their successful import indicates a complete installation.
Module import success in Python is the standard validation check for package installation integrity across all distribution methods.

direct_checks: verify conda is available and executable in the agent's environment
verify Bioconda channel is accessible and queryable
script_runs: conda install -c bioconda pyteomics completes without error
file_exists: verify Python site-packages contains pyteomics directory after installation
script_runs: Python script imports pyteomics.mass, pyteomics.pepxml, pyteomics.mzid without ImportError
output_matches_reference: verify each module import returns a module object with __name__ attribute matching the expected module path
expert_review: Assess whether the installed version and dependency resolution from Bioconda matches upstream source (github:levitsky/pyteomics) in functionality for the three tested modules
Confirm that no runtime errors or missing optional dependencies prevent the three core module imports from succeeding in a clean conda environment

cmd: conda install -y -c bioconda pyteomics && python -c "import pyteomics.mass; import pyteomics.pepxml; import pyteomics.mzid; import pyteomics; print('All imports successful'); print('Pyteomics version:', pyteomics.__version__)"

title: Extend Pyteomics installation to Bioconda and verify conda-installed package imports
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Use conda to install Pyteomics from the Bioconda channel with the command `conda install -c bioconda pyteomics`. 2. After installation completes, launch a Python interpreter and attempt to import pyteomics.mass. 3. Attempt to import pyteomics.pepxml. 4. Attempt to import pyteomics.mzid. 5. If all three imports execute without errors, record the success and the Python and Pyteomics versions in an installation verification report.
